stateDefinitions.empty: No merged lesson and no indexed open lesson pull request maps to the outcome.
stateDefinitions.inReview: At least one open lesson pull request maps to the outcome and has not completed quorum and adjudication.
stateDefinitions.published: At least one merged, publicly available CC BY 4.0 lesson maps to the outcome.

domains.title: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method
Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
Stage III · Treatment, lifespan, and continuity
Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

outcomes.title: Psychiatry's field and boundaries
outcomes.outcome: Explain how psychiatry studies mind, brain, person, relationship, culture, and system while distinguishing academic learning from clinical authority.
outcomes.summary: Explain how psychiatry studies mind, brain, person, relationship, culture, and system while distinguishing academic learning from clinical authority.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Description, inference, and interpretation
outcomes.outcome: Distinguish a person's report, another person's report, direct observation, inference, classification, and causal explanation.
outcomes.summary: Distinguish a person's report, another person's report, direct observation, inference, classification, and causal explanation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Competing explanatory models
outcomes.outcome: Compare biological, psychological, relational, social, cultural, and disability models by the questions they answer, evidence they use, and limits they retain.
outcomes.summary: Compare biological, psychological, relational, social, cultural, and disability models by the questions they answer, evidence they use, and limits they retain.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: History, power, and rights
outcomes.outcome: Analyze how institutions, racism, colonialism, gender, sexuality, stigma, and survivor movements have shaped psychiatric knowledge and care.
outcomes.summary: Analyze how institutions, racism, colonialism, gender, sexuality, stigma, and survivor movements have shaped psychiatric knowledge and care.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Person, recovery, and classification
outcomes.outcome: Evaluate useful and harmful uses of classification while preserving personhood, strengths, neurodiversity, recovery goals, and the right to participate in decisions.
outcomes.summary: Evaluate useful and harmful uses of classification while preserving personhood, strengths, neurodiversity, recovery goals, and the right to participate in decisions.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Cells, circuits, and networks
outcomes.outcome: Relate cellular signalling, circuits, large-scale networks, and behaviour without treating one scale as a complete explanation.
outcomes.summary: Relate cellular signalling, circuits, large-scale networks, and behaviour without treating one scale as a complete explanation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Neural signalling and plasticity
outcomes.outcome: Explain synaptic signalling, neuromodulation, learning, and plasticity with explicit timescale and causal limits.
outcomes.summary: Explain synaptic signalling, neuromodulation, learning, and plasticity with explicit timescale and causal limits.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Development, genetics, and epigenetics
outcomes.outcome: Appraise how development, genetic variation, and environmentally responsive regulation contribute probabilistically rather than deterministically.
outcomes.summary: Appraise how development, genetic variation, and environmentally responsive regulation contribute probabilistically rather than deterministically.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Stress, endocrine, immune, and metabolic systems
outcomes.outcome: Construct a bounded model of interacting stress, endocrine, immune, and metabolic processes and identify where clinical claims exceed evidence.
outcomes.summary: Construct a bounded model of interacting stress, endocrine, immune, and metabolic processes and identify where clinical claims exceed evidence.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Sleep and circadian biology
outcomes.outcome: Explain sleep regulation and circadian timing and predict how disruption can affect cognition, emotion, and physical health.
outcomes.summary: Explain sleep regulation and circadian timing and predict how disruption can affect cognition, emotion, and physical health.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Neuropsychological systems
outcomes.outcome: Use attention, memory, language, executive, social-cognitive, and motor systems to interpret a fictional pattern of strengths and difficulties.
outcomes.summary: Use attention, memory, language, executive, social-cognitive, and motor systems to interpret a fictional pattern of strengths and difficulties.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Neuroscience methods and causal inference
outcomes.outcome: Compare lesion, stimulation, electrophysiology, imaging, genetic, pharmacologic, and computational evidence by the inferences each design can support.
outcomes.summary: Compare lesion, stimulation, electrophysiology, imaging, genetic, pharmacologic, and computational evidence by the inferences each design can support.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Mind–brain model integration
outcomes.outcome: Build and critique a multi-level mind–brain account that states entities, relations, assumptions, predictions, and unresolved alternatives.
outcomes.summary: Build and critique a multi-level mind–brain account that states entities, relations, assumptions, predictions, and unresolved alternatives.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Developmental principles
outcomes.outcome: Explain continuity, change, timing, plasticity, and person–environment transactions across the life course.
outcomes.summary: Explain continuity, change, timing, plasticity, and person–environment transactions across the life course.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method
outcomes.moduleTitle: Development, relationships, and the life course

outcomes.title: Attachment and caregiving
outcomes.outcome: Compare attachment and caregiving models while separating observed relationship patterns from deterministic predictions.
outcomes.summary: Compare attachment and caregiving models while separating observed relationship patterns from deterministic predictions.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method
outcomes.moduleTitle: Development, relationships, and the life course

outcomes.title: Cognition, language, and identity
outcomes.outcome: Trace interacting changes in cognition, language, emotion, identity, and moral understanding across development.
outcomes.summary: Trace interacting changes in cognition, language, emotion, identity, and moral understanding across development.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method
outcomes.moduleTitle: Development, relationships, and the life course

outcomes.title: Adversity, protection, and resilience
outcomes.outcome: Evaluate evidence about adversity and protection without treating exposure as destiny or resilience as an individual obligation.
outcomes.summary: Evaluate evidence about adversity and protection without treating exposure as destiny or resilience as an individual obligation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method
outcomes.moduleTitle: Development, relationships, and the life course

outcomes.title: Family, peers, culture, and institutions
outcomes.outcome: Analyze how family, peers, education, work, migration, culture, and structural conditions shape developmental opportunities.
outcomes.summary: Analyze how family, peers, education, work, migration, culture, and structural conditions shape developmental opportunities.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method
outcomes.moduleTitle: Development, relationships, and the life course

outcomes.title: Life-course formulation
outcomes.outcome: Revise a fictional formulation across four ages while preserving uncertainty, strengths, turning points, and cohort context.
outcomes.summary: Revise a fictional formulation across four ages while preserving uncertainty, strengths, turning points, and cohort context.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method
outcomes.moduleTitle: Development, relationships, and the life course

outcomes.title: Setting, role, and consent
outcomes.outcome: Prepare an accessible simulated encounter by clarifying role, purpose, privacy, consent, communication needs, and limits.
outcomes.summary: Prepare an accessible simulated encounter by clarifying role, purpose, privacy, consent, communication needs, and limits.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Open and focused inquiry
outcomes.outcome: Move from an open invitation to focused questions without replacing the person's narrative with a checklist.
outcomes.summary: Move from an open invitation to focused questions without replacing the person's narrative with a checklist.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Chronology, function, strengths, and goals
outcomes.outcome: Construct a time course that connects experiences with function, strengths, priorities, context, and change from baseline.
outcomes.summary: Construct a time course that connects experiences with function, strengths, priorities, context, and change from baseline.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Mental-status observation
outcomes.outcome: Describe a time-bound mental-status examination while separating observation from inference and avoiding dehumanizing shorthand.
outcomes.summary: Describe a time-bound mental-status examination while separating observation from inference and avoiding dehumanizing shorthand.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Communication access and interpreters
outcomes.outcome: Adapt a simulated interview for language, sensory, cognitive, developmental, and communication differences using appropriate support.
outcomes.summary: Adapt a simulated interview for language, sensory, cognitive, developmental, and communication differences using appropriate support.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Alliance, rupture, and repair
outcomes.outcome: Recognize a simulated relational rupture, name its possible contribution, and attempt an accountable repair.
outcomes.summary: Recognize a simulated relational rupture, name its possible contribution, and attempt an accountable repair.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Boundaries and documentation
outcomes.outcome: Produce respectful documentation that is necessary, source-labelled, uncertainty-aware, and protected against inappropriate disclosure.
outcomes.summary: Produce respectful documentation that is necessary, source-labelled, uncertainty-aware, and protected against inappropriate disclosure.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Feedback and calibration
outcomes.outcome: Use transcript annotation, observer feedback, and self-assessment to identify one communication strength, one error, and a specific next practice target.
outcomes.summary: Use transcript annotation, observer feedback, and self-assessment to identify one communication strength, one error, and a specific next practice target.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Frequency, burden, and service use
outcomes.outcome: Interpret incidence, prevalence, burden, and service-use estimates with their population, period, sampling, and measurement limits.
outcomes.summary: Interpret incidence, prevalence, burden, and service-use estimates with their population, period, sampling, and measurement limits.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Measurement, validity, and invariance
outcomes.outcome: Evaluate reliability, validity, responsiveness, measurement invariance, missing data, and instrument rights for a proposed measure.
outcomes.summary: Evaluate reliability, validity, responsiveness, measurement invariance, missing data, and instrument rights for a proposed measure.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Diagnostic and prognostic evidence
outcomes.outcome: Interpret sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, likelihood ratios, calibration, and decision thresholds in context.
outcomes.summary: Interpret sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, likelihood ratios, calibration, and decision thresholds in context.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Study designs and causal claims
outcomes.outcome: Compare trials, observational studies, qualitative research, natural experiments, and causal diagrams by the questions they can answer.
outcomes.summary: Compare trials, observational studies, qualitative research, natural experiments, and causal diagrams by the questions they can answer.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Effects, harms, and heterogeneity
outcomes.outcome: Appraise absolute effects, uncertainty, clinical importance, heterogeneity, adverse outcomes, conflicts, and selective reporting.
outcomes.summary: Appraise absolute effects, uncertainty, clinical importance, heterogeneity, adverse outcomes, conflicts, and selective reporting.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Measurement-informed learning and care
outcomes.outcome: Design a fictional longitudinal measurement plan that tracks symptoms, function, quality of life, physical health, harms, preference, and equity without surrendering judgment to a score.
outcomes.summary: Design a fictional longitudinal measurement plan that tracks symptoms, function, quality of life, physical health, harms, preference, and equity without surrendering judgment to a score.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

outcomes.title: Problem representation
outcomes.outcome: Build a concise, source-labelled problem representation that preserves time course, function, context, strengths, and uncertainty.
outcomes.summary: Build a concise, source-labelled problem representation that preserves time course, function, context, strengths, and uncertainty.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Classification literacy
outcomes.outcome: Use ICD and DSM-related terminology critically without reproducing proprietary criteria or treating a code as a person.
outcomes.summary: Use ICD and DSM-related terminology critically without reproducing proprietary criteria or treating a code as a person.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Differential diagnosis and Bayesian updating
outcomes.outcome: Construct and reprioritize medical, neurologic, substance-related, developmental, psychiatric, and contextual alternatives as evidence changes.
outcomes.summary: Construct and reprioritize medical, neurologic, substance-related, developmental, psychiatric, and contextual alternatives as evidence changes.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Causal and maintenance formulation
outcomes.outcome: Connect predisposing, precipitating, perpetuating, protective, and structural factors in a revisable causal or maintenance model.
outcomes.summary: Connect predisposing, precipitating, perpetuating, protective, and structural factors in a revisable causal or maintenance model.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Culture, strengths, and meaning
outcomes.outcome: Integrate explanatory models, identity, spirituality, culture, power, goals, and strengths without stereotyping or diagnostic overshadowing.
outcomes.summary: Integrate explanatory models, identity, spirituality, culture, power, goals, and strengths without stereotyping or diagnostic overshadowing.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Communicating and updating a formulation
outcomes.outcome: Explain a working formulation collaboratively, state confidence and alternatives, invite correction, and define evidence that would prompt revision.
outcomes.summary: Explain a working formulation collaboratively, state confidence and alternatives, invite correction, and define evidence that would prompt revision.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Medical and neurologic alternatives
outcomes.outcome: Generate prioritized medical, neurologic, medication, substance, sleep, and physiologic alternatives for a fictional psychiatric presentation.
outcomes.summary: Generate prioritized medical, neurologic, medication, substance, sleep, and physiologic alternatives for a fictional psychiatric presentation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Medicine, neurology, investigations, and physical health

outcomes.title: Integrated history and medication reconciliation
outcomes.outcome: Organize medical, neurologic, developmental, medication, substance, reproductive, sleep, and family information with source and chronology.
outcomes.summary: Organize medical, neurologic, developmental, medication, substance, reproductive, sleep, and family information with source and chronology.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Medicine, neurology, investigations, and physical health

outcomes.title: Examination and investigations
outcomes.outcome: Select and interpret bounded physical, neurologic, cognitive, laboratory, and imaging information while avoiding indiscriminate testing.
outcomes.summary: Select and interpret bounded physical, neurologic, cognitive, laboratory, and imaging information while avoiding indiscriminate testing.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Medicine, neurology, investigations, and physical health

outcomes.title: Delirium and cognitive change
outcomes.outcome: Distinguish acute attention and awareness change from longer-term cognitive and psychiatric patterns and identify urgent escalation in simulation.
outcomes.summary: Distinguish acute attention and awareness change from longer-term cognitive and psychiatric patterns and identify urgent escalation in simulation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Medicine, neurology, investigations, and physical health

outcomes.title: Physical health, prevention, and treatment burden
outcomes.outcome: Analyze preventable physical-health inequity, medication burden, lifestyle constraints, access, and diagnostic overshadowing.
outcomes.summary: Analyze preventable physical-health inequity, medication burden, lifestyle constraints, access, and diagnostic overshadowing.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Medicine, neurology, investigations, and physical health

outcomes.title: Integrated physical-health plan
outcomes.outcome: Construct an academic monitoring and coordination plan that states priorities, interactions, uncertainty, roles, and when licensed care is required.
outcomes.summary: Construct an academic monitoring and coordination plan that states priorities, interactions, uncertainty, roles, and when licensed care is required.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Medicine, neurology, investigations, and physical health

outcomes.title: Recognizing urgency
outcomes.outcome: Identify fictional patterns that require emergency assessment while distinguishing urgency from prediction certainty.
outcomes.summary: Identify fictional patterns that require emergency assessment while distinguishing urgency from prediction certainty.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Self-harm and suicide formulation
outcomes.outcome: Build a dynamic, person-centred safety formulation using current and changing factors, protective resources, uncertainty, and direct escalation.
outcomes.summary: Build a dynamic, person-centred safety formulation using current and changing factors, protective resources, uncertainty, and direct escalation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Violence, agitation, and safety
outcomes.outcome: Analyze agitation or threatened violence without equating diagnosis with dangerousness and propose proportionate, least-restrictive simulated responses.
outcomes.summary: Analyze agitation or threatened violence without equating diagnosis with dangerousness and propose proportionate, least-restrictive simulated responses.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Intoxication, withdrawal, delirium, and catatonia
outcomes.outcome: Discriminate time-critical fictional presentations and identify the limits of remote or unsupervised response.
outcomes.summary: Discriminate time-critical fictional presentations and identify the limits of remote or unsupervised response.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Safeguarding, capacity, and rights
outcomes.outcome: Reason through safeguarding, decision-making ability, supported choices, confidentiality, and local-law uncertainty in a fictional crisis.
outcomes.summary: Reason through safeguarding, decision-making ability, supported choices, confidentiality, and local-law uncertainty in a fictional crisis.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Collaborative crisis planning and learning
outcomes.outcome: Construct a simulation-only crisis and recovery plan with the person's priorities, supporters, access barriers, warning signs, local routes, follow-up, and post-event learning.
outcomes.summary: Construct a simulation-only crisis and recovery plan with the person's priorities, supporters, access barriers, warning signs, local routes, follow-up, and post-event learning.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Psychosis phenomenology and language
outcomes.outcome: Describe unusual beliefs, perceptions, thought, communication, self-experience, negative symptoms, cognition, and function without ridicule or premature explanation.
outcomes.summary: Describe unusual beliefs, perceptions, thought, communication, self-experience, negative symptoms, cognition, and function without ridicule or premature explanation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Psychosis differential and context
outcomes.outcome: Differentiate psychiatric, mood-related, substance, medication, neurologic, medical, sleep, developmental, trauma-related, and culturally situated explanations.
outcomes.summary: Differentiate psychiatric, mood-related, substance, medication, neurologic, medical, sleep, developmental, trauma-related, and culturally situated explanations.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Course, outcomes, and recovery
outcomes.outcome: Explain heterogeneous courses and recovery without presenting population patterns as an individual's destiny.
outcomes.summary: Explain heterogeneous courses and recovery without presenting population patterns as an individual's destiny.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Antipsychotic evidence and harms
outcomes.outcome: Compare evidence for benefits, adverse effects, uncertainty, monitoring, preference, and withdrawal without issuing personal treatment advice.
outcomes.summary: Compare evidence for benefits, adverse effects, uncertainty, monitoring, preference, and withdrawal without issuing personal treatment advice.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Psychological, family, peer, and social interventions
outcomes.outcome: Compare psychosocial, family, peer, rehabilitation, housing, and community approaches by goals, evidence, access, and burden.
outcomes.summary: Compare psychosocial, family, peer, rehabilitation, housing, and community approaches by goals, evidence, access, and burden.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Collaborative longitudinal reasoning
outcomes.outcome: Build a fictional longitudinal plan that integrates meaning, safety, physical health, treatment choices, rights, social goals, and review points.
outcomes.summary: Build a fictional longitudinal plan that integrates meaning, safety, physical health, treatment choices, rights, social goals, and review points.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Mood phenomenology and function
outcomes.outcome: Describe depressive, elevated, irritable, mixed, psychotic, cognitive, sleep, energy, and functional changes with time course and source.
outcomes.summary: Describe depressive, elevated, irritable, mixed, psychotic, cognitive, sleep, energy, and functional changes with time course and source.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Mood differential and longitudinal course
outcomes.outcome: Distinguish unipolar, bipolar-spectrum, medical, neurologic, medication, substance, grief, trauma, and contextual alternatives using longitudinal evidence.
outcomes.summary: Distinguish unipolar, bipolar-spectrum, medical, neurologic, medication, substance, grief, trauma, and contextual alternatives using longitudinal evidence.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Safety and uncertainty in mood presentations
outcomes.outcome: Integrate self-harm, suicide, impulsivity, vulnerability, safeguarding, protective factors, and uncertainty without false precision.
outcomes.summary: Integrate self-harm, suicide, impulsivity, vulnerability, safeguarding, protective factors, and uncertainty without false precision.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Medication and interventional evidence
outcomes.outcome: Compare medication and interventional options by indication, benefit, harms, switching risk, monitoring, access, and stopping considerations.
outcomes.summary: Compare medication and interventional options by indication, benefit, harms, switching risk, monitoring, access, and stopping considerations.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Psychological and social evidence
outcomes.outcome: Compare psychological, family, behavioural, peer, occupational, and social approaches by phase, preference, mechanism, and evidence.
outcomes.summary: Compare psychological, family, behavioural, peer, occupational, and social approaches by phase, preference, mechanism, and evidence.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Longitudinal mood formulation
outcomes.outcome: Revise a fictional mood formulation over time and construct a collaborative relapse, recovery, physical-health, and outcome-monitoring plan.
outcomes.summary: Revise a fictional mood formulation over time and construct a collaborative relapse, recovery, physical-health, and outcome-monitoring plan.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Anxiety, obsession, trauma, and dissociation phenomenology
outcomes.outcome: Describe fear, worry, panic, compulsions, trauma responses, avoidance, dissociation, bodily arousal, and function without assuming one cause.
outcomes.summary: Describe fear, worry, panic, compulsions, trauma responses, avoidance, dissociation, bodily arousal, and function without assuming one cause.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, and dissociative presentations

outcomes.title: Differential and boundary reasoning
outcomes.outcome: Differentiate anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, dissociative, medical, substance, developmental, mood, psychotic, and culturally patterned experiences.
outcomes.summary: Differentiate anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, dissociative, medical, substance, developmental, mood, psychotic, and culturally patterned experiences.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, and dissociative presentations

outcomes.title: Learning, cognitive, and contextual models
outcomes.outcome: Compare conditioning, cognitive, memory, attachment, stress, and social-context models by their predictions and limits.
outcomes.summary: Compare conditioning, cognitive, memory, attachment, stress, and social-context models by their predictions and limits.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, and dissociative presentations

outcomes.title: Psychotherapy evidence and adaptation
outcomes.outcome: Compare exposure-based, cognitive, trauma-focused, supportive, and other relevant approaches by indication, process, harms, preference, and adaptation.
outcomes.summary: Compare exposure-based, cognitive, trauma-focused, supportive, and other relevant approaches by indication, process, harms, preference, and adaptation.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, and dissociative presentations

outcomes.title: Medication evidence and limits
outcomes.outcome: Compare medication evidence, adverse effects, dependence or withdrawal concerns, comorbidity, monitoring, and patient preference.
outcomes.summary: Compare medication evidence, adverse effects, dependence or withdrawal concerns, comorbidity, monitoring, and patient preference.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, and dissociative presentations

outcomes.title: Trauma-aware and culturally responsive planning
outcomes.outcome: Construct a fictional plan that supports choice, pacing, safety, communication access, culture, function, and recovery without requiring disclosure of personal trauma.
outcomes.summary: Construct a fictional plan that supports choice, pacing, safety, communication access, culture, function, and recovery without requiring disclosure of personal trauma.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations
outcomes.moduleTitle: Anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma-related, and dissociative presentations

outcomes.title: Substance-use spectrum and harm reduction
outcomes.outcome: Describe use, intoxication, withdrawal, dependence, harms, protective practices, and recovery using non-stigmatizing language.
outcomes.summary: Describe use, intoxication, withdrawal, dependence, harms, protective practices, and recovery using non-stigmatizing language.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Substance pharmacology and time course
outcomes.outcome: Relate major substance classes to acute and chronic effects, interactions, tolerance, withdrawal, and medical urgency.
outcomes.summary: Relate major substance classes to acute and chronic effects, interactions, tolerance, withdrawal, and medical urgency.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Assessment and differential reasoning
outcomes.outcome: Integrate chronology, quantity uncertainty, route, context, function, co-occurring conditions, prescribed medicines, and toxicology limits.
outcomes.summary: Integrate chronology, quantity uncertainty, route, context, function, co-occurring conditions, prescribed medicines, and toxicology limits.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Psychological, medication, peer, and social interventions
outcomes.outcome: Compare intervention options by substance, goal, evidence, harms, access, preference, and stage of change.
outcomes.summary: Compare intervention options by substance, goal, evidence, harms, access, preference, and stage of change.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Systems, policy, and stigma
outcomes.outcome: Analyze how criminalization, supply, marketing, inequality, housing, trauma, service design, and stigma shape risk and access.
outcomes.summary: Analyze how criminalization, supply, marketing, inequality, housing, trauma, service design, and stigma shape risk and access.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Behavioural addictions and contested boundaries
outcomes.outcome: Appraise a proposed behavioural addiction by impairment, reinforcement, alternatives, classification evidence, moral judgment, and risk of over-medicalization.
outcomes.summary: Appraise a proposed behavioural addiction by impairment, reinforcement, alternatives, classification evidence, moral judgment, and risk of over-medicalization.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Personality and relational function
outcomes.outcome: Formulate enduring and context-sensitive patterns in self, emotion, relationships, coping, and function without reducing a person to a trait label.
outcomes.summary: Formulate enduring and context-sensitive patterns in self, emotion, relationships, coping, and function without reducing a person to a trait label.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Eating and feeding presentations
outcomes.outcome: Integrate nutritional, medical, developmental, psychological, cultural, and social evidence and recognize fictional medical urgency.
outcomes.summary: Integrate nutritional, medical, developmental, psychological, cultural, and social evidence and recognize fictional medical urgency.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Sleep presentations
outcomes.outcome: Differentiate insomnia, circadian, breathing-related, movement-related, parasomnia, substance, medication, mood, and environmental contributors.
outcomes.summary: Differentiate insomnia, circadian, breathing-related, movement-related, parasomnia, substance, medication, mood, and environmental contributors.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Somatic distress, pain, and fatigue
outcomes.outcome: Construct a both-and formulation that validates suffering, keeps medical alternatives open, and avoids false divisions between physical and psychological.
outcomes.summary: Construct a both-and formulation that validates suffering, keeps medical alternatives open, and avoids false divisions between physical and psychological.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Sexual, gender, and bodily context
outcomes.outcome: Discuss sexual function, gender-related care, reproductive context, body image, disability, and medication effects with consent, appropriate language, and role boundaries.
outcomes.summary: Discuss sexual function, gender-related care, reproductive context, body image, disability, and medication effects with consent, appropriate language, and role boundaries.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Integrated relational and functional plan
outcomes.outcome: Build a fictional plan that coordinates medical safety, psychological formulation, relationships, behaviour, environment, strengths, and patient-defined goals.
outcomes.summary: Build a fictional plan that coordinates medical safety, psychological formulation, relationships, behaviour, environment, strengths, and patient-defined goals.
outcomes.domainTitle: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

outcomes.title: Psychopharmacology principles
outcomes.outcome: Connect receptor, circuit, pharmacodynamic, and clinical models while identifying where mechanism does not predict patient-important benefit.
